Commands mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project. mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server. mkdocs build - Build the documentation site. mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit. mkdocs gh-deploy - Deploy to Github Pages Project layout 1 2 3 4 mkdocs.yml # The configuration file. docs/ index.md # The documentation homepage. ... # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.text: Youtube-dl Firefox Addon Firefox extension to download videos/audio from Youtube and others video websites using youtube-dl Table of Contents Description Prerequisites Installation How to use this addon Command line version Contributing Troubleshooting License Description This is a firefox addon helping you downloads youtube videos or audio using youtube-dl . Simply use the addon while browsing youtube to download the audio or video. You can configure youtube-dl to download only audio, or convert into any desired format (mp3,wav,...) using FFMPEG, or download full video in any available quality. You can even download full Youtube playlists ! The addon only display most commun formats : m4a (Native audio format for YT), mp3 (standard audio format) and mp4 (native video format for YT) NOTE It works with almost all markdown flavours (the below blank line matters). Prerequisites Needs Mozilla Firefox installed. Needs Python installed. Don't forget to check "ADD Python to Path" and to "Disable PATH length limit" This has only been tested on Windows. Mac & Linux users might have to make some changes Installation Clone/Download this repository Run the setup_win.bat file as ADMIN to install youtube-dl and necessary dependancies Install the add on from Firefox Addons Website . Restart firefox and it should work How to use this addon Go to any youtube page Press the addon's logo in the toolbar, then select the format you want (m4a, mp3, mp4) The video/audio will be downloaded in the background After the download is finished, you will get a notification saying the download has finished. Note: The default download location is the "Download" folder. If you want to change that, check youtube-dl's configuration Command line version If you want to use youtube-dl from command line you need to add FFMPEG to system environnement variables. Go to "control Panel" > "System and Security" > "System" Go to "Advanced System Settings" Go to "Environement Variables" Now find "PATH" in the "System Variables" section and click "edit" Click "New" and write "C:\youtube_dl\FFMPEG\bin" Re-open your command prompt to commit changes Contributing Fork it! Create your feature branch: git checkout -b my-new-feature Commit your changes: git commit -am 'Add some feature' Push to the branch: git push origin my-new-feature Submit a pull request :D Troubleshooting If you experience youtube-dl errors message, it is highly possible that Youtube changed something making the program not working anymore. However, a fix is usually found within 24h so you just have to upgrade your youtube-dl version by using this command in a windows shell : 1 pip install --upgrade youtube-dl License This program is Youtube Dl Firefox Addon. Copyright (C) 2020 Thibaut Decombe. Youtube Dl Firefox Addon is available under the MIT license. See the LICENSE file for more info.
docs.title: About

docs.text: This is a firefox addon helping you downloads youtube videos or audio using youtube-dl . Simply use the addon while browsing youtube to download the audio or video. You can configure youtube-dl to download only audio, or convert into any desired format (mp3,wav,...) using FFMPEG, or download full video in any available quality. You can even download full Youtube playlists ! The addon only display most commun formats : m4a (Native audio format for YT), mp3 (standard audio format) and mp4 (native video format for YT) NOTE It works with almost all markdown flavours (the below blank line matters).
docs.title: Description

docs.text: Needs Mozilla Firefox installed. Needs Python installed. Don't forget to check "ADD Python to Path" and to "Disable PATH length limit" This has only been tested on Windows. Mac & Linux users might have to make some changes

docs.text: Clone/Download this repository Run the setup_win.bat file as ADMIN to install youtube-dl and necessary dependancies Install the add on from Firefox Addons Website . Restart firefox and it should work

docs.text: Go to any youtube page Press the addon's logo in the toolbar, then select the format you want (m4a, mp3, mp4) The video/audio will be downloaded in the background After the download is finished, you will get a notification saying the download has finished. Note: The default download location is the "Download" folder. If you want to change that, check youtube-dl's configuration
docs.title: How to use this addon

docs.text: If you want to use youtube-dl from command line you need to add FFMPEG to system environnement variables. Go to "control Panel" > "System and Security" > "System" Go to "Advanced System Settings" Go to "Environement Variables" Now find "PATH" in the "System Variables" section and click "edit" Click "New" and write "C:\youtube_dl\FFMPEG\bin" Re-open your command prompt to commit changes

docs.text: Fork it! Create your feature branch: git checkout -b my-new-feature Commit your changes: git commit -am 'Add some feature' Push to the branch: git push origin my-new-feature Submit a pull request :D

docs.text: If you experience youtube-dl errors message, it is highly possible that Youtube changed something making the program not working anymore. However, a fix is usually found within 24h so you just have to upgrade your youtube-dl version by using this command in a windows shell : 1 pip install --upgrade youtube-dl

docs.text: Essentials Introduction Git is a decentralized version management software: It allows to store data remotely but also locally. Git does not rely on a centralized server, but uses a peer-to-peer connection system. The developed computer code is stored not only on the computer of each project contributor, but it can also be stored on a dedicated server (Remote). Git init Initialize a .git repository. It can be used to convert an existing, unversioned project to a Git repository or initialize a new, empty repository. 1 git init Git config Setup user details. Git will associate them with locally created commits. 1 2 git config - -global user . email "myemail@gmail.com" git config - -global user . name "MyUsername" To see current config : 1 git config --list Result 1 2 user.name = MyUsername user.email = myemail@gmail.com Git status Get informations about the current git directory (Untracked/new/changed/removed files) 1 git status Git add/reset Add/remove files from git tracking Git add Add to tracking (Stage changes) Single File 1 git add readme.md Info readme.md file is staged Every HTML Files 1 git add *.html Info Every HTML files are staged All Files 1 git add --all Tip Every available files are staged. You should git status first to make sure you are not tracking unwanted files. Git reset Remove files from git tracking Single File 1 git reset HEAD readme.md Info readme.md file is not tracked anymore (But changes made to the file remain unchanged) All Files 1 git reset HEAD Info Cancels all last git add (Unstage all) Git commit Commit changes to git. Only tracked files are committed. You can add files and commit with the same command line Commit 1 git commit -m "Mon premier commit" Add + Commit 1 git commit -a -m "Premiere modif" Git log Git logging to see details about commits. Git log : Basic operation Oneline : Use One-line Display File Specific : Display commits associated with a specific file Git log 1 2 git log # Shows last commits git log -n 2 # Shows the last 2 commits Oneline 1 git log --oneline Exemple 1 2 3 4 1653f44 Create README.md 8f3add2 Add requirements.txt 1a5c42c New theme material 3af103e First commit File specific 1 git log -p readme.md Git diff Display what changed since last commit 1 git diff Gitignore Setup files to ignore when versioning with a .gitignore file 1 2 3 *.tmp # Ignore tout les fichiers .tmp log.txt # Ignore le fichier log.txt tmp/* # Ignore tout les fichiers du dossier tmp Sources Git Docs ↩
docs.title: Git Essentials

docs.text: Advanced Git branch In this exemple we take : branch_name = igloo Create 1 git branch igloo Checkout Move to a specific branch Move to Igloo 1 git checkout igloo Move to master 1 git checkout master Merge Merch branch with Master branch Merge 1 2 git checkout master git merge igloo Warning This might create a "Merge branch" commit if master was ahead of branch When master branch is ahead, use rebase Merge & Rebase history 1 2 3 4 git checkout igloo git rebase master git checkout master git merge igloo Info git rebase master : redesign branch now start from last master commit (even if he was ahead before) Igloo Branch history is added to master branch history Interactive 1 2 git checkout igloo git rebase -i master Tip pick = use commit squash = use commit, but meld into previous commit fixup = like "squash", but discard the commit's log message 1 2 git checkout master git merge igloo Delete Delete branch after successful merge 1 git branch -d igloo Undo commits & changes Revert a commit (Doesn't change history) Revert every changes made in a commit with a new 'revert' commit Full Commit 1 git revert 7921a1e Specific File 1 git revert 7921a1e index.html Revert changes made after last commit Revert every changes made after last commit. 3 modes available : Soft : Keep changes, Unstage changes Mixed(default) : Keep changes, Unstage changes Hard : Delete changes Mixed 1 git reset --mixed Soft 1 git reset --soft Hard 1 git reset --hard Warning 1 You permanently lose every changes made locally since last commit Revert to specific commit Use Case #1 : How to reset last terrible commit (Last good commit is 32e4fc4)) ? Use Case #2 : How to rewrite history since commit 47e9cf6 ? Case #1 1 2 3 git reset 32e4fc4 # Edit files as necessary git commit -a -m "Fix terrible bug" Info Go in the past, make changes to fix bug and then commit to rewrite history. Case #2 1 2 git reset 47e9cf6 git commit -a -m "History rewritten, changes retains" Info We go back to commit 47e9cf6. History is removed but files are not modified locally. Then we commit changes, history between commit and master has been rewritten. Manipulate history Amend Combine few commits Default (Confirm commit message) 1 git commit --amend Change commit message 1 git commit --amend -m "New commit message" Warning Only do this locally on commits we haven't push to remote yet ! Stash Use Stash to save current changes in a temporary memory. Create Default 1 git stash Warning Untracked changes are not stashed Always use git status first Stash Untracked 1 git stash -u Info Untracked changes are also stashed ! Rename Stash 1 git stash save My Stash cool name Info Stash is saved with the name "My stash cool name" List 1 git stash list Exemple Return a stash list 1 2 stash@ { 0 } : On master : My stash cool name stash@ { 1 } : WIP on master : Remove IE9 support Apply Bring changes back Most Recent Stash 1 git stash apply Info Apply most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash apply stash@ { 1 } Info Apply stash n°1 Delete Delete remaining stash Most Recent Stash 1 git stash drop Info Drop most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash drop stash@ { 1 } Info Drop stash n°1 Show Display changes saved in stash Most Recent Stash 1 git stash show Info Show most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash show stash@ { 1 } Info Show stash n°1 More details 1 git stash show -p stash@ { 1 } Tip Add more details with -p Stash to another branch Use Case : Move all current changes to a new branch 1 2 git stash git stash branch iglooStash Info Automatically create & move you to 'iglooStash' branch Move changes to this branch Remove latest stash
docs.title: **Advanced**

docs.text: Fork, Issues & Pull Request Might be usefull to have a SSH connection setup (Use ssh-keygen -t rsa -C "youremail@gmail.com" and add the public key to your Github account) Close an issue you can close an issue with a special syntaxe in your bug solving commit 1 git commit -a -m "fixes #12 Solve Issue n°12" Tip You can replace “fixes” keyword with any in this list : “Fixed”, “Fix”, “Closes”, “Closed”, or “Close” Fork a repository Allow user to work on his own version of a repository Validation is needed to add changes to the main project via a pull request Danger Never do commits to your fork master. Always work on a branch Master branch should only be used to synchronize your fork with owner repository Initial Fork Just go to a Github repository and click the “Fork” option This also import repository history Now clone this, do your changes and push it to a remote branch 1 2 3 4 5 git clone https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/youtube-dl.git git branch readme-fix git checkout readme-fix git commit -a -m "Wonderful readmefix.md" git push origin readme-fix Tip Always create a new branch because you don't want to do pull request on master since the main project master might have changed in between. Upstream Fork If you want to update your fork with owner changes Checkout master branch 1 2 git remote add upstream https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl.git git remote -v Info You should see this : 1 2 3 4 origin https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/youtube-dl.git ( fetch ) origin https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/youtube-dl.git ( push ) upstream https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl.git ( fetch ) upstream https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl.git ( push ) Now you need to collect code from the upstream remote : 1 2 git fetch upstream git branch -a Info You should get a remotes/upstream/master Now merge with your local master : 1 git merge upstream/master Pull request Do this on Github with the “Pull Request” option Repo owner will get a notification and accept or not your request A pull request conversation is created to discuss about potential changes to make
docs.title: Fork & Pull Request

docs.text: Might be usefull to have a SSH connection setup (Use ssh-keygen -t rsa -C "youremail@gmail.com" and add the public key to your Github account)
docs.title: Fork, Issues & Pull Request

docs.text: you can close an issue with a special syntaxe in your bug solving commit 1 git commit -a -m "fixes #12 Solve Issue n°12" Tip You can replace “fixes” keyword with any in this list : “Fixed”, “Fix”, “Closes”, “Closed”, or “Close”
docs.title: Close an issue

docs.text: Allow user to work on his own version of a repository Validation is needed to add changes to the main project via a pull request Danger Never do commits to your fork master. Always work on a branch Master branch should only be used to synchronize your fork with owner repository
docs.title: Fork a repository

docs.text: Just go to a Github repository and click the “Fork” option This also import repository history Now clone this, do your changes and push it to a remote branch 1 2 3 4 5 git clone https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/youtube-dl.git git branch readme-fix git checkout readme-fix git commit -a -m "Wonderful readmefix.md" git push origin readme-fix Tip Always create a new branch because you don't want to do pull request on master since the main project master might have changed in between.
docs.title: Initial Fork

docs.text: If you want to update your fork with owner changes Checkout master branch 1 2 git remote add upstream https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl.git git remote -v Info You should see this : 1 2 3 4 origin https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/youtube-dl.git ( fetch ) origin https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/youtube-dl.git ( push ) upstream https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl.git ( fetch ) upstream https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl.git ( push ) Now you need to collect code from the upstream remote : 1 2 git fetch upstream git branch -a Info You should get a remotes/upstream/master Now merge with your local master : 1 git merge upstream/master
docs.title: Upstream Fork

docs.text: Do this on Github with the “Pull Request” option Repo owner will get a notification and accept or not your request A pull request conversation is created to discuss about potential changes to make
docs.title: Pull request

docs.text: Remote Save all your git history to a remote (Github, Gitlab, ...) Create The template is : git remote add <remote_name> <adress> Github HTTPS 1 git remote add origin https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git Warning Untracked changes are not stashed Always use git status first Github SSH 1 git remote add origin git@github.com:User/UserRepo.git Info Untracked changes are also stashed ! Local Remote 1 2 3 4 5 cd C:/path/to/local/remote git init --bare cd MyGitProject git remote add origin C:/path/to/local/remote Info Local folder can be use as a remote Clone The template is : git clone <remote_link> <local_name> Default 1 git clone https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git Info Clone repository in a 'sound-play' folder Rename 1 git clone https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git custom_name Info Clone repository in a 'custom_name' folder Depth limit 1 git clone --depth 5 https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git Warning Only keep the last 5 commits in local history Push The template is : git push <remote_name> <local_branch_name> Best practice to use remote_name = origin for the main remote (it's Github default for ex) Push branch 1 2 git push origin master git push origin igloo Remove remote branch 1 git remote rm igloo Pull The template is : git pull <remote_name> <remote_branch_name> Pull branch 1 git pull origin master Warning Might create a "Merge branch" commit if local is ahead of remote Use rebase to avoid that Pull branch & Rebase History 1 git pull --rebase origin master Setup rebase as default 1 git config --global branch.autosetuprebase always List List every remotes or branches Remote List 1 git remote -v Info List every remote available Remote Branch List 1 git branch -r Info List every remote branches available Rename 1 git remote rename origin lol Info Now the remote name is 'lol' instead of 'origin' You can use git remote -v to see that
docs.title: Git Remote

docs.text: Save all your git history to a remote (Github, Gitlab, ...)
docs.title: Remote

docs.text: The template is : git remote add <remote_name> <adress> Github HTTPS 1 git remote add origin https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git Warning Untracked changes are not stashed Always use git status first Github SSH 1 git remote add origin git@github.com:User/UserRepo.git Info Untracked changes are also stashed ! Local Remote 1 2 3 4 5 cd C:/path/to/local/remote git init --bare cd MyGitProject git remote add origin C:/path/to/local/remote Info Local folder can be use as a remote
docs.title: Create

docs.text: The template is : git clone <remote_link> <local_name> Default 1 git clone https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git Info Clone repository in a 'sound-play' folder Rename 1 git clone https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git custom_name Info Clone repository in a 'custom_name' folder Depth limit 1 git clone --depth 5 https://github.com/UnknownPlatypus/sound-play.git Warning Only keep the last 5 commits in local history
docs.title: Clone

docs.text: The template is : git push <remote_name> <local_branch_name> Best practice to use remote_name = origin for the main remote (it's Github default for ex) Push branch 1 2 git push origin master git push origin igloo Remove remote branch 1 git remote rm igloo
docs.title: Push

docs.text: The template is : git pull <remote_name> <remote_branch_name> Pull branch 1 git pull origin master Warning Might create a "Merge branch" commit if local is ahead of remote Use rebase to avoid that Pull branch & Rebase History 1 git pull --rebase origin master Setup rebase as default 1 git config --global branch.autosetuprebase always
docs.title: Pull

docs.text: List every remotes or branches Remote List 1 git remote -v Info List every remote available Remote Branch List 1 git branch -r Info List every remote branches available
docs.title: List

docs.text: 1 git remote rename origin lol Info Now the remote name is 'lol' instead of 'origin' You can use git remote -v to see that
docs.title: Rename

docs.text: Stash Use Stash to save current changes in a temporary memory. Create Default 1 git stash Warning Untracked changes are not stashed Always use git status first Stash Untracked 1 git stash -u Info Untracked changes are also stashed ! Rename Stash 1 git stash save My Stash cool name Info Stash is saved with the name "My stash cool name" List 1 git stash list Exemple Return a stash list 1 2 stash@ { 0 } : On master : My stash cool name stash@ { 1 } : WIP on master : Remove IE9 support Apply Bring changes back Most Recent Stash 1 git stash apply Info Apply most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash apply stash@ { 1 } Info Apply stash n°1 Delete Delete remaining stash Most Recent Stash 1 git stash drop Info Drop most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash drop stash@ { 1 } Info Drop stash n°1 Show Display changes saved in stash Most Recent Stash 1 git stash show Info Show most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash show stash@ { 1 } Info Show stash n°1 More details 1 git stash show -p stash@ { 1 } Tip Add more details with -p Stash to another branch Use Case : Move all current changes to a new branch 1 2 git stash git stash branch iglooStash Info Automatically create & move you to 'iglooStash' branch Move changes to this branch Remove latest stash
docs.title: Git Stash

docs.text: Use Stash to save current changes in a temporary memory.
docs.title: Stash

docs.text: Default 1 git stash Warning Untracked changes are not stashed Always use git status first Stash Untracked 1 git stash -u Info Untracked changes are also stashed ! Rename Stash 1 git stash save My Stash cool name Info Stash is saved with the name "My stash cool name"
docs.title: Create

docs.text: 1 git stash list Exemple Return a stash list 1 2 stash@ { 0 } : On master : My stash cool name stash@ { 1 } : WIP on master : Remove IE9 support
docs.title: List

docs.text: Bring changes back Most Recent Stash 1 git stash apply Info Apply most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash apply stash@ { 1 } Info Apply stash n°1
docs.title: Apply

docs.text: Delete remaining stash Most Recent Stash 1 git stash drop Info Drop most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash drop stash@ { 1 } Info Drop stash n°1
docs.title: Delete

docs.text: Display changes saved in stash Most Recent Stash 1 git stash show Info Show most recent stash Specific Stash 1 git stash show stash@ { 1 } Info Show stash n°1 More details 1 git stash show -p stash@ { 1 } Tip Add more details with -p
docs.title: Show

docs.text: Use Case : Move all current changes to a new branch 1 2 git stash git stash branch iglooStash Info Automatically create & move you to 'iglooStash' branch Move changes to this branch Remove latest stash
docs.title: Stash to another branch
